message:
So last week the power cut out at 8 p.m., and I confidently announced to my family, "Don't worry, I'm *totally* prepared for this." Then I spent twenty minutes in the dark searching for flashlights I was *sure* we had. Found one with dead batteries. Found another with... no batteries at all, just the ghost of good intentions.

My phone was at 12%. My partner's was at 8%. We had no power bank. The fridge was basically a warm box of regret at that point.

Turns out "I think we're ready" and "we're actually ready" are two wildly different things.

The good news? It's genuinely not hard to close that gap. A few working flashlights (with fresh batteries actually *in* them), a couple of cheap power banks tucked in a drawer, and a plan for keeping the fridge organized so nothing panics if the power blinks out—that's really the difference between "well, this is annoying" and "well, this is *fine*."

No drama. No spending hours prepping. Just... actual stuff that works when you need it.

What's your blackout wild card—the thing that always catches you off guard?
